title: complete state-backed gate migration

tasks.title: RED: failing store-level test for updateArtifacts cache invalidation (AC9)
tasks.verification: RED test written + confirmed failing: 'invalidates change cache after updateArtifacts (AC9)' — invalidateChangeMock called 0 times (expected 1). Test asserts updateArtifacts must call invalidateChange(changeId).

tasks.title: GREEN: add invalidateChange(changeId) to updateArtifacts (AC9, root cause of stale-contract symptom)
tasks.verification: GREEN: added invalidateChange(changeId) to updateArtifacts before return. AC9 test 'invalidates change cache after updateArtifacts' now passes. Only remaining failure is the worktree_guard_enforce spec assertion (project.json guard disabled during dev; task 10 reverts).

tasks.title: RED: failing unit test for readAgreement Temporal-first ordering (AC8)
tasks.verification: RED test written + confirmed failing: 'adv_contract_mint uses Temporal-fresh agreement over stale disk (AC8)' — disk-stale agreement (1 AC) used instead of Temporal-fresh (2 ACs); itemCount 2, expected 3. Proves readAgreement is disk-first.

tasks.title: GREEN: make readAgreement Temporal-first by delegating to readArtifact (AC8)
tasks.verification: GREEN: readAgreement now delegates to readArtifact(store, change.id, 'agreement') — Temporal-first → disk → archive. Removed unused readFile/join imports. AC8 test passes; full contract.test.ts suite green; typecheck clean (exit 0). No import cycle (change.ts does not import contract.ts).

tasks.title: RED: failing test for state-backed acceptance gate (no-disk) through workflow signal path (AC1, AC5)
tasks.verification: RED test written + confirmed failing: 'completes acceptance state-backed with no pre-existing disk file (AC1, AC5, AC7)' — acceptance gate is 'stuck' (expected 'done'). Proves legacy disk inspectArtifactActivity path fails when no executive-summary.md exists on disk (Temporal-only store). Proof present in state.documents.executiveSummary + state.artifacts metadata.

tasks.title: GREEN: state-backed acceptance non-recovery branch behind new patch marker (AC1, AC3, AC7, AC2, C2, C3)
tasks.verification: GREEN core: added STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH marker + new state-backed acceptance branch in workflows.ts (reads proof from state.documents.executiveSummary + state.artifacts metadata, no disk inspect; AC7 materializes executive-summary.md + acceptance.md to disk via writeArtifactActivity for archive bundle). Added stateBackedAcceptanceProof() helper to gate-readiness.ts (deterministic, no node externals). Extended writeArtifactActivity proxy kind union with executiveSummary. Legacy ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH disk path preserved for old-history replay (AC3). Updated 2 existing acceptance tests to state-backed model + reframed stale-hash test to state-missing-content. PASS: workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts (all), gate-readiness.test.ts, workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts, workflows.test.ts, RED AC1/AC5/AC7 test now green. typecheck exit 0. Only expected failure: worktree_guard_enforce spec assertion (project.json guard disabled during dev; task 10 reverts).

tasks.title: Test: recovery-path + replay-determinism coverage (AC2, AC3)
tasks.verification: AC2 + AC3 coverage. AC2: existing gate.ts poisoned-history acceptance recovery tests pass unchanged (recovery path uses disk inspectArtifactActivity + hash comparison, untouched per C2/C4). AC3: added structural replay-determinism guard 'checks state-backed acceptance patch before the legacy disk-inspect patch' — asserts wf.patched(STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH) precedes wf.patched(ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH) so old histories fall through to legacy branch deterministically. Both pass (only unrelated worktree_guard_enforce spec assertion fails, reverted by task 10).

tasks.title: Test: legacy on-disk acceptance compatibility + archive-bundle executive-summary materialization (AC4, AC7)
tasks.verification: AC4 + AC7 archive coverage. Added 2 tests to archive.test.ts: (1) createInRepoArchive includes executive-summary.md + acceptance.md from source change dir in bundle (AC7 — confirms the readdir-copy path picks up files materialized by the state-backed acceptance branch); (2) archives cleanly when source change dir absent (AC4 legacy/no-disk safe). Confirmed createInRepoArchive uses readdir sibling-copy from sourceChangeDir, validating the AC7 design decision (state-backed branch writes executive-summary.md to active dir at acceptance time). Both pass (only unrelated worktree_guard_enforce assertion fails).

tasks.title: Docs: update fixGateArtifactReadiness recovery note + workflows.ts inline comments (AC6)
tasks.verification: AC6 docs parity. Updated docs/temporal-recovery.md: (1) added 'State-backed acceptance' note to the poisoned-history-recovery distinction section explaining the non-recovery acceptance path is now state-backed under STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH (state.documents.executiveSummary proof, AC7 disk materialization for archive bundle, legacy disk-inspect branch retained for replay only, recovery path unchanged per C12); (2) extended 'Stuck ... gates' section title + symptom + recovery procedure to include acceptance / executive-summary.md. workflows.ts inline comments (added in tk-8c1f75224607) already explain state-backed vs recovery division + patch-marker rationale. typecheck exit 0.

tasks.title: Verification gate: full suite + invariants + revert guard flip (C1, C6)
tasks.verification: C1 + C6 verification gate PASS. Reverted project.json worktree_guard_enforce false→true (task 10 guard flip). Then: pnpm run check exit 0 (typecheck + test-isolation + lockfile-policy + lint + format:check all clean; formatted 2 touched files); full suite pnpm test --maxWorkers=4 exit 0 (all tests pass, including previously-expected worktree_guard_enforce spec assertion now green after revert); pnpm run build exit 0 (worker bundle workflows.js 72.77KB compiled successfully, no Webpack errors). State-backed acceptance migration complete: all 15 contract items (AC1-9 + C1-6) covered. Recovery path + inspectArtifactActivity untouched (C2/C4). Replay-safe via STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH marker (C3/AC3).

subagent_reports.topic: Validate state-backed acceptance gate migration design

sources.label: Temporal TypeScript SDK — Versioning with Patching
sources.summary: Each substantive code-change is a logical branch via a unique patch ID; patches are feature-flags for replay safety. Three-step lifecycle: patched() -> deprecatePatch() -> remove. Confirms separate patch IDs per logical change are the canonical approach.

sources.label: Temporal encyclopedia — Patching
sources.summary: A Patch defines a logical branch in a Workflow for a specific change; one patch per substantive code-sequence change.

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/workflows.ts (current code)
sources.summary: Existing patches: DISCOVERY_CONTRACT_READINESS_PATCH (v1), ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH (v1), STATE_BACKED_GATE_ARTIFACT_PROOF_PATCH (v1). The non-acceptance branch already uses stateBackedArtifactEvidence under the STATE_BACKED patch; acceptance is explicitly carved out via `artifactKind !== "acceptance"`. Lines 803-832 (acceptance projection write + state.documents.acceptance population) are inside ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH branch. Lines 833-883 (inspectArtifactActivity for executive-summary) follow.

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/gate-readiness.ts:97-146 (stateBackedArtifactEvidence)
sources.summary: Pure function. Reads state.documents[artifactKind] for content, state.artifacts[artifactKind] for {path, contentHash} metadata, emits GateArtifactEvidence. Already used by proposal/discovery/design. acceptance artifactKind has the same {path, contentHash} metadata shape (gate-readiness.ts:201-228 confirms executiveSummary lives in state.artifacts with same fields).

sources.label: plugin/src/temporal/gate-readiness.ts:172-228 (acceptanceContractBlockers)
sources.summary: L1 readiness check (always-on) already requires state.artifacts.executiveSummary.{path,contentHash}. State-backed metadata is the source of truth at L1. The break is purely the L2 inspectArtifactActivity at workflows.ts:833-883.

sources.label: plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/no-disk-writes-invariant.test.ts
sources.summary: Structural test enforcing the temporal store's create() and updateArtifacts() never write artifact content to disk. Confirms disk file for executive-summary.md does not exist post-write in production.

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/change.ts readArtifact
sources.summary: Three-tier read: Temporal state.documents -> disk active dir -> archive bundle. adv_change_show include.executiveSummary works correctly without disk file while workflow is reachable. Falls through to archive bundle for archived changes.

sources.label: plugin/src/archive/archive.ts createArchive / createInRepoArchive
sources.summary: Archive bundle materialization is `readdir(sourceChangeDir)` + per-entry `readFile` -> atomicWriteFile to the bundle. NO state.documents fallback. Any artifact missing from the source change directory at archive time is silently NOT copied to the bundle (catch block is swallow-only for outer readdir; per-entry errors push to warnings array).

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/change.read-artifact.test.ts
sources.summary: Test demonstrates and depends on archive bundle containing executive-summary.md for post-archive reads. Confirms that bundle is the eventual source-of-truth for executive-summary once Temporal retention expires.

sources.label: plugin/src/tools/gate.ts (acceptance recovery path)
sources.summary: Recovery path uses writeArtifactActivity (writes acceptance.md to disk) then inspectArtifactActivity for both executive-summary and acceptance. Disk inspection here is intentional and orthogonal to the production non-recovery path. C12 of removePositionalArtifactApi mandates preservation.

sources.label: CHANGELOG.md (executive-summary intent)
sources.summary: "executive-summary.md is a new optional narrative artifact ... for release notes, changelogs, and user-facing communication." "Archive bundle copy logic (automatic — picks up executive-summary.md via existing readdir-all-files copy)" — this assumed-existence model is what's now broken once production write path stopped writing the file.

subagent_reports.architecture_assessment:
Design is directionally correct (state-backed predicate, replay-safe patch marker, recovery-path preservation) but has one CAUTION-level scope gap: the change closes the acceptance gate-readiness break but does NOT close the matching archive-bundle materialization break introduced by the same upstream cause (no-disk-writes invariant). Findings per dimension:

1) REPLAY-DETERMINISM (PASS) — `wf.patched("state-backed-acceptance-proof-v1")` as a NEW patch ID is the canonical Temporal pattern. Each substantive command-sequence change is a separate logical patch (Temporal docs: "A Patch defines a logical branch in a Workflow for a specific change"). Expanding the existing STATE_BACKED_GATE_ARTIFACT_PROOF_PATCH would conflate two independent migrations whose histories overlap differently — wrong, because histories that recorded STATE_BACKED for non-acceptance gates but pre-date the acceptance migration would mis-branch on replay. Following fixGateArtifactReadiness's pattern verbatim is correct.

2) STATE-VS-DISK SEMANTICS (PASS) — `stateBackedArtifactEvidence(state, "acceptance", "executiveSummary", now)` works directly: state.documents.executiveSummary holds content; state.artifacts.executiveSummary holds {path, contentHash} metadata, identical shape to proposal/discovery/design. gate-readiness.ts L1 acceptanceContractBlockers already validates this metadata shape (lines 201-228), so the helper drops in without modification. No new abstraction needed.

3) RECOVERY-PATH PRESERVATION (PASS) — gate.ts:340-419 is a separate code path (poisoned-history compatibility), uses writeArtifactActivity to materialize disk artifacts at recovery time, then inspectArtifactActivity to verify. Scope boundary is correct: the disk inspection there serves a different purpose (poisoned-history disk verification per C12 of removePositionalArtifactApi) and is unaffected by the production-path migration. C4 "Single-purpose change" is sound.

4) BLOCK-ORDERING CORRECTNESS (PASS WITH NOTE) — Lines 803-832 (writeArtifactActivity for `acceptance` projection + state.documents.acceptance population) must stay because: (a) acceptance.md is a DIFFERENT artifact from executive-summary.md — it's the gate-enforcement projection (per CHANGELOG line 431: "acceptance.md is the gate-enforcement projection verified by inspectArtifactActivity; executive-summary.md is a field-style artifact"); (b) the recovery path AND archive bundle materialization both rely on acceptance.md existing on disk. NOTE: This means the no-disk-writes invariant has an explicit acceptance.md carve-out today; the design correctly does not touch this. Lines 833-883 (executiveSummary inspectArtifactActivity) are exactly the right surgical surface.

5) CONSUMER-IMPACT (CAUTION) — Archive bundle materialization (plugin/src/archive/archive.ts:600-622 `createArchive`, 733-752 `createInRepoArchive`) uses `readdir(sourceChangeDir)` + `readFile` to copy sibling files into the bundle. There is NO state.documents fallback. Pre-design, executive-summary.md was guaranteed on disk because gate completion called writeArtifactActivity (well, no it wasn't — the symptom proves this assumption is already false today). Post-design: state.documents.executiveSummary exists in Temporal state, disk file still does not exist, archive bundle copy will silently skip executive-summary.md. Downstream consequences: (a) post-archive `readArtifact` for executiveSummary returns null once Temporal retention expires (archive bundle lacks the file); (b) CHANGELOG/release-notes generation that depends on executive-summary content breaks; (c) the change.read-artifact.test.ts:87-110 fallback-to-archive-bundle path becomes unreachable for new changes. The design's Avoidance A3 ("Archive-bundle artifact materialization ... unchanged") is technically respected, but the precondition that A3 silently relied upon ("files exist on disk at archive time") is what removePositionalArtifactApi already broke. The design closes the gate-readiness symptom but leaves the archive-bundle symptom. This is the same composition gap as the original two changes shipped 2026-05-28.

6) PATCH-MARKER CORRECTNESS (PASS) — Per Temporal canonical guidance (https://docs.temporal.io/develop/typescript/versioning#patching), each substantive change is its own patch. The two patches in-flight (ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH from a prior change, STATE_BACKED_GATE_ARTIFACT_PROOF_PATCH from fixGateArtifactReadiness) have already been recorded against existing histories. Adding a third (`state-backed-acceptance-proof-v1`) lets histories that recorded ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH=v1 but PRE-date this migration replay deterministically through the old inspectArtifactActivity branch, while new histories take the state-backed branch. Reusing or expanding an existing marker is documented as an anti-pattern when the change shape differs.

blockers: Archive-bundle materialization scope gap: createArchive() and createInRepoArchive() in plugin/src/archive/archive.ts copy artifacts from sourceChangeDir via readdir/readFile only. Since removePositionalArtifactApi removed disk writes for executiveSummary content, the bundle will be missing executive-summary.md for any change that travels the new non-recovery happy path. This breaks post-archive readArtifact() fallback (returns null after Temporal retention expires) and any downstream release-notes consumer. The design's Avoidance A3 keeps archive materialization out of scope, but the precondition (file exists on disk at archive time) is already broken. Either (a) expand scope to materialize executive-summary.md from state.documents at archive time, or (b) explicitly accept the gap and add a follow-up change before any new change archives — and document/test that post-archive executiveSummary is unavailable until that follow-up ships.
validation.notes: All other dimensions PASS. The Temporal patch-id choice, state-backed predicate reuse, recovery-path scope boundary, and block-ordering (lines 803-832 stay outside the patch because acceptance projection + state.documents.acceptance are still needed for recovery + archive readdir) are all correct. The CAUTION is a composition-gap echo of the original symptom: the design closes the gate-readiness consumer but leaves the archive-bundle consumer broken under the same upstream invariant.

subagent_reports.recommendation: Revise design before planning gate. Two acceptable paths: (A — PREFERRED, scope-expand) extend the design to also materialize state.documents.executiveSummary -> bundle/executive-summary.md inside the same `state-backed-acceptance-proof-v1` patch branch (right next to the existing line-803 writeArtifactActivity for acceptance.md), OR add an archive-time materialization in createArchive() that reads state.documents from the workflow snapshot. (B — scope-defer) explicitly add an avoidance A7 acknowledging the archive-bundle gap, document it in CHANGELOG, add a regression test asserting current behavior, and create a follow-up change (`materializeExecutiveSummaryAtArchive`) as a blocker for any new change archive. Path A is preferred because it composes cleanly with the surgery already happening at workflows.ts:803-832, follows the same write-to-disk-at-gate-completion pattern already in use for acceptance.md (which has the same archive-bundle requirement and uses exactly this approach), and avoids re-introducing a known unrecoverable consumer break. Path A also keeps the C1 constraint intact in spirit (no GENERAL disk dual-write restoration; only artifact-specific archive-time materialization gated to the acceptance moment). Other than this gap, the design is sound: ready-for-planning once the archive-bundle consumer is addressed.
follow_ups: If design adopts Path A: add an AC7 — "executive-summary.md exists in archive bundle after non-recovery acceptance completes; verified by regression test that runs the no-disk happy path through archive and asserts bundle contains executive-summary.md with state.documents content."
If design adopts Path B: add a `materializeArtifactsAtArchiveFromState` change to the backlog and link it as a blocker for next archive operation.

Verify the design content (was truncated and design gate is still pending in change state — design.md was not located on disk). Confirm the actual design artifact reflects this validation feedback before completing the design gate.

Consider whether acceptance.md (lines 803-832) is itself sound long-term: writing it to disk solely for inspectArtifactActivity (recovery) + archive readdir copy is the inverse of state-backed. Out of scope here, but a future change `materializeAcceptanceProjectionAtArchive` could complete the Temporal-proof transition.

wisdom.content: readAgreement (tools/contract.ts:91-106) is disk-first, Temporal-fallback: reads agreement.md from disk, only falls back to change.documents?.agreement when disk is empty/missing. In the Temporal-canonical architecture this is backwards — Temporal state.documents should be the canonical source, disk the legacy fallback. It did NOT cause the stale-contract symptom here (disk absent → fallback fired), but it's the same architectural class this change addresses. Separate finding: stale 12-item contract was a read-after-write gap on change.documents between adv_change_update and an immediately-following non-dry adv_contract_mint force:true; dryRun re-read returned correct 13 items, proving state was fresh and only the persisted contract was stale. Mitigation: when re-minting after adv_change_update, run a dryRun first to confirm freshness, or re-mint twice. Candidate follow-up: invert readAgreement (and sibling artifact readers in contract.ts / change.ts) to Temporal-first.

proposal.approval_evidence: User approved proposal + agreement inline ('approve'). Problem statement, root cause analysis, success criteria, and signed agreement (6 ACs, 6 constraints, 6 avoidances) persisted.

discovery.approval_evidence:
Discovery: confirmed architectural facts.

L1 (gate-readiness.ts:201-228 acceptanceContractBlockers): runs ALWAYS, requires state.artifacts.executiveSummary.path + contentHash metadata. Currently satisfied by write flow (signal populates metadata).

L2 (workflows.ts:833-883 under ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH): calls inspectArtifactActivity to verify disk content + hash match. THIS is the actual break — disk file is no longer written by Temporal store (no-disk-writes-invariant.test.ts enforces). Hash in state.artifacts is from in-memory content, no file exists at path.

Recovery path (gate.ts:343-414): preserved untouched per C2/C4. Uses inspectArtifactActivity against disk written by writeArtifactActivity at recovery time. C12 of removePositionalArtifactApi.

Write path confirmed: store-temporal/changes.ts fireContentSignalsSequentially → executiveSummaryUpdatedSignal → state.documents.executiveSummary; updateArtifactMetadataSignal → state.artifacts.executiveSummary.{path, contentHash} computed from in-memory content. No disk write.

Existing test surface: gate-readiness.test.ts already covers state-backed approach for proposal/discovery/design. Acceptance section exists with tests at lines 442-504. workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts mocks inspectArtifactActivity for full workflow simulation.

Fix surface: workflows.ts:833-883 only. The L1 readiness check + metadata signal flow is unchanged.</notes>
</invoke>

design.approval_evidence:
Design finalized with independent validation + 3 folded-in findings.

Validator (adv-researcher): CAUTION — 5/6 dimensions pass. Consumer-impact dimension flagged archive-bundle materialization gap (createArchive readdir copy would miss executive-summary.md). User approved Path A (scope-expand) → AC7 archive-time materialization. Replay-determinism, state-vs-disk semantics, recovery-path preservation, block-ordering, patch-marker correctness all PASS (validated against Temporal patching docs).

Folded-in findings (user-approved):
- AC7: writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary) inside new patched branch for archive-bundle presence
- AC8: readAgreement (contract.ts) disk-first → Temporal-first (lone outlier vs canonical readArtifact)
- AC9: updateArtifacts (store-temporal/changes.ts) missing invalidateChange(changeId) — ROOT CAUSE of stale-contract symptom, confirmed live this session (3 mint cycles needed due to stale changeCache after adv_change_update)

Contract minted: 15 items (AC1-9 + C1-6), hash 1d34d5b9.

Fix surfaces:
- workflows.ts: STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH wrapping lines 833-883
- contract.ts: readAgreement delegates to readArtifact (circular-import fallback documented)
- store-temporal/changes.ts: one invalidateChange(changeId) call in updateArtifacts

Replay determinism preserved via new versioned patch marker (4th in file). Recovery path (gate.ts:343-414) untouched per C2/C4.

planning.approval_evidence: Prep contract approved by user ('approve'). 10 tasks, TDD-ordered across 3 fix surfaces (workflows.ts state-backed acceptance + patch marker + AC7 ES materialization; contract.ts readAgreement Temporal-first; store-temporal/changes.ts updateArtifacts cache invalidation). All 15 contract items mapped (AC1-9 + C1-6). Recovery path + inspectArtifactActivity untouched (C2/C4). No spec delta (existing advance-workflow req envelope covers both approaches). Task 10 reverts project.json guard-disable before release.

execution.approval_evidence: All 10 tasks complete (TDD red→green across 3 fix surfaces). Full verification: pnpm run check exit 0, pnpm test --maxWorkers=4 exit 0, pnpm run build exit 0. AC1-9 + C1-6 covered. State-backed acceptance migration: workflows.ts STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH branch (state.documents.executiveSummary proof, no disk inspect; AC7 disk materialization for archive bundle); contract.ts readAgreement Temporal-first (delegates to readArtifact); store-temporal/changes.ts updateArtifacts invalidateChange (AC9 root-cause fix). Recovery path + inspectArtifactActivity untouched (C2/C4). Guard flip reverted.

artifact_evidence.compatibility_reason: Live deployed plugin is the stale pre-fix dist (AGENTS.md source-vs-dist gotcha): the running Temporal worker uses the legacy disk-inspect acceptance path that this very change replaces. The non-recovery state-backed path exists in source (tests green) but is not yet loaded by the running plugin host.

items.text: **AC1 — Non-recovery acceptance state-backed.** When `state.documents.executiveSummary` is non-empty AND `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.{path,contentHash}` are set, `adv_gate_complete gateId: "acceptance"` advances regardless of pre-existing disk-file presence. Verified by integration test that creates a change, runs it through gates without prior disk-writes for executive-summary, asserts acceptance completes.

**AC2 — Recovery path disk inspection preserved.** When acceptance gate completion uses `recoveryMode: "poisoned_history"`, `inspectArtifactActivity` continues to verify disk content against `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.contentHash`. Verified by replay test exercising the existing recovery flow.

**AC3 — Replay safety.** A versioned patch marker is added around the workflow-side change. Old histories replay deterministically; new histories use the new path. Verified by deterministic-replay test.

**AC4 — Legacy compatibility.** Existing changes that pre-date `removePositionalArtifactApi` and have on-disk acceptance artifacts continue to complete acceptance cleanly. Verified by a regression test fixture.

**AC5 — Test coverage.** New regression test asserts no-disk acceptance completion through the full workflow signal path. Existing `fixGateArtifactReadiness` pattern tests extended to cover acceptance.

**AC6 — Documentation parity.** The `fixGateArtifactReadiness` recovery note (acceptance carve-out) is updated to reflect the completed migration. Inline comments in `workflows.ts` explain state-backed vs recovery-path division.

**AC7 — Archive-bundle executive-summary materialization.** At acceptance gate completion (inside the new patched branch), `state.documents.executiveSummary` is written to disk at `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.path` via `writeArtifactActivity`, mirroring the existing acceptance.md projection write at workflows.ts:803. Ensures `createArchive`/`createInRepoArchive` include executive-summary.md in the bundle. Verified by integration test through acceptance + archive asserting `_executiveSummary` resolves from bundle.

**AC8 — readAgreement Temporal-first.** `readAgreement` in `tools/contract.ts` reads Temporal `state.documents.agreement` FIRST, falling back to disk then archive — matching `readArtifact` ordering. Ideally delegates to `readArtifact(store, change.id, "agreement")`. Verified by unit test: Temporal-fresh + disk-stale agreement → minted contract uses Temporal content.

**AC9 — updateArtifacts cache invalidation.** `store-temporal/changes.ts updateArtifacts` calls `invalidateChange(changeId)` after the content-signal fan-out (before return), matching `save`/`close`/`refresh`/bulk-close. Ensures a `store.changes.get(changeId)` immediately following `adv_change_update` returns fresh `state.documents`/`state.artifacts` rather than stale cached content. Verified by a store-level test: update artifact content, then immediate `get`, assert returned `documents[kind]` equals the new content (RED today: returns stale cached value).

**C1 — No production-write-flow disk dual-write.** MUST NOT restore disk writes for `agreement`/`design`/`executiveSummary`/`acceptance` in the artifact-update write path (`store-temporal/changes.ts` fireContentSignalsSequentially). `no-disk-writes-invariant.test.ts` continues to hold. AC7's archive-time materialization is a separate lifecycle (one-time at acceptance gate), identical in pattern to the existing acceptance.md write at workflows.ts:803.

**C2 — `inspectArtifactActivity` interface unchanged.** Continues to exist and remains used by recovery path + archive bundle materialization. Only its USE SITE in the non-recovery acceptance gate changes.
**C3 — Workflow replay determinism.** All workflow code changes gated behind a `patched` marker. The new `writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary)` (AC7) sits inside the new patched branch.

**C4 — Bounded scope.** Scope limited to: (a) acceptance non-recovery readiness predicate, (b) archive-bundle composition (AC7), (c) `readAgreement` Temporal-first fix (AC8), (d) `updateArtifacts` cache-invalidation fix (AC9). Do NOT touch the recovery path. Do NOT touch `inspectArtifactActivity`'s implementation. Do NOT broaden AC8/AC9 into a sweeping reader/cache refactor — AC8 is `readAgreement` only; AC9 is the single missing `invalidateChange` call in `updateArtifacts`.
**C5 — Worktree isolation (P32).** All edits via the change worktree; no trunk edits.
**C6 — Test-driven.** RED evidence first; GREEN implementation; targeted + full test pass before review.

rows.evidence: workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts 'completes acceptance state-backed with no pre-existing disk file (AC1, AC5, AC7)' — acceptance gate reaches 'done' from state.documents.executiveSummary + state.artifacts metadata with no disk file. New STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH branch in workflows.ts reads proof from state via stateBackedAcceptanceProof().

gate.test.ts 'poisoned-history acceptance recovery writes disk projection' + recovery suite pass unchanged. git diff 8408c1cf..HEAD shows gate.ts and _recovery-writers.ts have ZERO diff — recovery path inspectArtifactActivity + hash comparison fully preserved per C2/C4.

workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts 'checks state-backed acceptance patch before the legacy disk-inspect patch (AC3)' — asserts wf.patched(STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH) precedes wf.patched(ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH) so old histories fall through to legacy branch deterministically. workflow-bundle-boundary.test.ts + workflows.test.ts pass.

archive.test.ts 'archives cleanly when the source change dir is absent (legacy/no-disk safe)' + 'includes executive-summary.md and acceptance.md from the source change dir in the bundle'. Legacy on-disk acceptance handled via same readdir sibling-copy path in createInRepoArchive.

New no-disk acceptance test through full workflow signal path (workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts). Existing acceptance pattern tests extended/updated to state-backed model (acceptance-projection test seeds state.documents; stale-hash test reframed to state-missing-content blocker). Full suite pnpm test --maxWorkers=4 exit 0.

docs/temporal-recovery.md updated: State-backed acceptance note added to poisoned-history-recovery distinction section; 'Stuck ... gates' section title+symptom+procedure extended to acceptance/executive-summary.md. workflows.ts inline comments explain state-backed vs recovery division + STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH rationale + deprecation plan.

State-backed acceptance branch materializes executive-summary.md + acceptance.md to active change dir via writeArtifactActivity (proxy kind union extended with executiveSummary). archive.test.ts confirms createInRepoArchive readdir-copy includes executive-summary.md in bundle. workflows.signal-handlers no-disk test asserts executive-summary.md readable on disk after acceptance.

contract.test.ts 'adv_contract_mint uses Temporal-fresh agreement over stale disk (AC8)' — Temporal-fresh agreement (3 contract items) used over stale disk (2 items). readAgreement now delegates to readArtifact(store, change.id, 'agreement') Temporal-first → disk → archive. No import cycle (change.ts does not import contract.ts). Unused readFile/join imports removed.

changes.test.ts 'invalidates change cache after updateArtifacts (AC9)' — invalidateChange(changeId) now called (was 0 in RED). Single invalidateChange call added before return in updateArtifacts, matching save/close/refresh/bulk-close. Root cause of stale-contract symptom.

No production-write disk dual-write added. fireContentSignalsSequentially unchanged. AC7 materialization is a separate one-time acceptance-gate lifecycle (mirrors existing acceptance.md write). no-disk-writes-invariant test passes in full suite (exit 0).
git diff 8408c1cf..HEAD shows activities.ts has ZERO diff — inspectArtifactActivity interface + implementation unchanged. Only its non-recovery acceptance use-site changed (now state-backed).

All workflow changes gated behind STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH marker. AC7 writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary) sits inside the new patched branch. AC3 patch-ordering test + workflow-bundle-boundary determinism guards pass.

git diff --stat 8408c1cf..HEAD: only 4 fix surfaces + tests + docs touched (9 files). Recovery path (gate.ts, _recovery-writers.ts) zero diff. inspectArtifactActivity (activities.ts) zero diff. AC8 limited to readAgreement (contract.ts +27 lines incl import). AC9 single invalidateChange call (changes.ts +9). Not broadened.

All edits made in change worktree /home/jon/.local/share/opencode/worktree/.../change/completeStateBackedGate on branch change/completeStateBackedGate. No trunk edits. Each task git-checkpointed in worktree.

TDD red→green across all 3 fix surfaces: AC9 (RED tk-846d, GREEN tk-9498), AC8 (RED tk-8699, GREEN tk-ecf0), AC1/5/7 (RED tk-926f, GREEN tk-8c1f). Each RED confirmed failing before GREEN. Targeted + full suite (exit 0) pass before review.

acceptanceCriteria: **AC1 — Non-recovery acceptance state-backed.** When `state.documents.executiveSummary` is non-empty AND `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.{path,contentHash}` are set, `adv_gate_complete gateId: "acceptance"` advances regardless of pre-existing disk-file presence. Verified by integration test that creates a change, runs it through gates without prior disk-writes for executive-summary, asserts acceptance completes.

**AC2 — Recovery path disk inspection preserved.** When acceptance gate completion uses `recoveryMode: "poisoned_history"`, `inspectArtifactActivity` continues to verify disk content against `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.contentHash`. Verified by replay test exercising the existing recovery flow.

**AC3 — Replay safety.** A versioned patch marker is added around the workflow-side change. Old histories replay deterministically; new histories use the new path. Verified by deterministic-replay test.

**AC4 — Legacy compatibility.** Existing changes that pre-date `removePositionalArtifactApi` and have on-disk acceptance artifacts continue to complete acceptance cleanly. Verified by a regression test fixture.

**AC5 — Test coverage.** New regression test asserts no-disk acceptance completion through the full workflow signal path. Existing `fixGateArtifactReadiness` pattern tests extended to cover acceptance.

**AC6 — Documentation parity.** The `fixGateArtifactReadiness` recovery note (acceptance carve-out) is updated to reflect the completed migration. Inline comments in `workflows.ts` explain state-backed vs recovery-path division.

**AC7 — Archive-bundle executive-summary materialization.** At acceptance gate completion (inside the new patched branch), `state.documents.executiveSummary` is written to disk at `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.path` via `writeArtifactActivity`, mirroring the existing acceptance.md projection write at workflows.ts:803. Ensures `createArchive`/`createInRepoArchive` include executive-summary.md in the bundle. Verified by integration test through acceptance + archive asserting `_executiveSummary` resolves from bundle.

**AC8 — readAgreement Temporal-first.** `readAgreement` in `tools/contract.ts` reads Temporal `state.documents.agreement` FIRST, falling back to disk then archive — matching `readArtifact` ordering. Ideally delegates to `readArtifact(store, change.id, "agreement")`. Verified by unit test: Temporal-fresh + disk-stale agreement → minted contract uses Temporal content.

**AC9 — updateArtifacts cache invalidation.** `store-temporal/changes.ts updateArtifacts` calls `invalidateChange(changeId)` after the content-signal fan-out (before return), matching `save`/`close`/`refresh`/bulk-close. Ensures a `store.changes.get(changeId)` immediately following `adv_change_update` returns fresh `state.documents`/`state.artifacts` rather than stale cached content. Verified by a store-level test: update artifact content, then immediate `get`, assert returned `documents[kind]` equals the new content (RED today: returns stale cached value).

documents.proposal:
# Complete state-backed gate migration

## Intent

Close the residual gap between `fixGateArtifactReadiness` (state-backed proposal/discovery/design) and `removePositionalArtifactApi` (removed disk writes for `agreement`/`design`/`executiveSummary`/`acceptance`). Result today: acceptance gate readiness blocks via `inspectArtifactActivity` requiring disk artifacts that the write flow no longer produces.

Architectural direction confirmed by user (2026-05-28): recent changes deliberately moved everything to be Temporal-proof, not disk-write. Acceptance is the last gate still depending on disk and must be migrated to state-backed evidence to complete the architectural transition.

## Symptom

Reproducible end-to-end. Demonstrated tonight (2026-05-28→29 UTC) on two toolbox changes (`fixOcE2eTestBugs`, `fixLauncherPostMigration`) shipped through 7-gate lifecycle:

1. `adv_change_update executiveSummary: "<content>"` returns success
2. `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.path` and `.contentHash` populated
3. `state.documents.executiveSummary` populated (via existing flow)
4. **Disk file at the metadata's `.path` is NOT created** — by design per `removePositionalArtifactApi`
5. `adv_gate_complete gateId: "acceptance"` blocks: `ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_MISSING — Artifact not found: <path>`
6. Manual workaround: write file to disk with content matching metadata's `contentHash` + re-fire `adv_change_update` → gate passes

Same pattern blocking pokeedge-web's `fixSubDollarLabels` (4 re-entries observed before the user shifted sessions).

## Root Cause

Two recent changes shipped same day (2026-05-28) and don't compose:

| Change | What it did | Side effect |
|---|---|---|
| `fixGateArtifactReadiness` | Migrated proposal/discovery/design readiness checks to read `state.documents` + `state.artifacts` (state-backed) | Deliberately kept acceptance on `inspectArtifactActivity` (disk-based) — assumption was disk writes would continue |
| `removePositionalArtifactApi` | Moved artifact content to `state.documents`; disk dual-write removed for `agreement`/`design`/`executiveSummary` | The artifact acceptance depends on is no longer written to disk |

`fixGateArtifactReadiness`'s assumption (acceptance keeps disk inspection because disk content is reliable) was invalidated by `removePositionalArtifactApi`'s removal of disk writes for the very artifact acceptance depends on. The two changes were shipped on the same day and the composition gap was not caught.

## Proposed direction

**Extend state-backed evidence to acceptance gate.** Apply the same `stateBackedArtifactEvidence` pattern from `fixGateArtifactReadiness` to the acceptance gate. Read `state.documents.executiveSummary` for content + `state.artifacts.executiveSummary` for metadata. Drop `inspectArtifactActivity` from the non-recovery acceptance path. Recovery path keeps `inspectArtifactActivity` per C12 of `removePositionalArtifactApi` (D12 of `addPerProjectOcWrapper` empirically verified `inspectArtifactActivity` is XDG-aware and disk-correct for poisoned-history scenarios).

This is the architectural completion: all four artifact-backed gates (proposal, discovery, design, acceptance) read Temporal as canonical. Disk inspection survives only on the recovery path where it serves a different purpose (verifying disk evidence when workflow projection is poisoned).

Restoring disk dual-write was considered and rejected as wrong-direction per confirmed architectural intent ("Temporal-proof, not disk write").

## LBP Targets

- Confirm parity between the four artifact-backed gates (proposal, discovery, design, acceptance) on the new state-backed path
- Confirm recovery-path disk inspection remains intact for poisoned-history scenarios (C12 of `removePositionalArtifactApi`)
- Confirm legacy change histories with on-disk acceptance artifacts still complete cleanly

## Scope

- `plugin/src/temporal/workflows.ts:880` (non-recovery acceptance path): replace `inspectArtifactActivity` call with `stateBackedArtifactEvidence("executiveSummary", state)` equivalent
- `plugin/src/temporal/workflows.ts:833` (recovery path): unchanged
- `plugin/src/temporal/gate-readiness.ts:201-225`: already metadata-only (path + contentHash); confirm semantics align with state-backed approach for other gates
- Add replay-safe patch marker similar to `STATE_BACKED_GATE_ARTIFACT_PROOF_PATCH` from `fixGateArtifactReadiness` (versioned for determinism)
- Regression test: stage a no-disk acceptance completion through the workflow boundary; assert it advances without `ARTIFACT_MISSING`
- Update the `fixGateArtifactReadiness` recovery note in documentation to remove the acceptance carve-out

## Success Criteria

- `adv_gate_complete gateId: "acceptance"` advances cleanly when `state.documents.executiveSummary` and `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.{path,contentHash}` are present, regardless of disk-file presence
- Acceptance-recovery path (poisoned-history) continues to use `inspectArtifactActivity` (C12 of `removePositionalArtifactApi` preserved)
- Full `pnpm test` passes; `pnpm run check`; `pnpm run build`
- Replay determinism preserved via versioned patch marker
- Existing change histories with on-disk acceptance artifacts continue to work (legacy compatibility)
- New regression test asserts no-disk acceptance completion through the workflow signal path
- All four artifact-backed gates (proposal, discovery, design, acceptance) now consistently treat Temporal as canonical on the non-recovery path

## Out of Scope

- Restoring disk writes for any artifact (rejected — contradicts architectural intent)
- `inspectArtifactActivity` itself (used elsewhere — recovery path, archive bundle materialization)
- Re-evaluating `fixGateArtifactReadiness`'s state-backed approach for proposal/discovery/design (already shipped and working)
- Migrating `inspectArtifactActivity` itself away from disk (recovery path needs disk for poisoned-history; architecturally correct)
- Toolbox-side workaround tooling (the manual disk-write + re-fire pattern works today as a stopgap)

## Recovery Note for affected changes (pre-this-change)

Until this ships, any change hitting `ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_MISSING` after the new write flow can be unblocked manually:

1. Fetch content via `adv_change_show changeId: <id> include: { executiveSummary: true }` — extract `_executiveSummary`
2. Write content to disk at the path in `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.path`
3. Re-fire `adv_change_update changeId: <id> executiveSummary: <same content>`
4. Retry `adv_gate_complete gateId: "acceptance"`

SHA256 of disk content will match the existing `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.contentHash` if step 1's content is used verbatim. After this change ships, the manual workaround becomes unnecessary.

## Reproducibility evidence

- Toolbox change `fixOcE2eTestBugs` (archived 2026-05-28, merge `beb1b0f`): blocked at acceptance; workaround applied
- Toolbox change `fixLauncherPostMigration` (archived 2026-05-29, merge `6de5faba`): blocked at acceptance; workaround applied
- pokeedge-web `fixSubDollarLabels`: 4 prior re-entries before user shifted sessions; agent diagnosis "poisoned-history" reached for `recoveryMode` escape — actually the same disk-write gap surfacing

## Composition lesson

When two architectural changes touch overlapping invariants (disk vs Temporal as canonical for artifact content), they should either:
- Ship as a single change with the composition explicit, OR
- Have each one's design call out and verify the other's assumptions

This is worth capturing as wisdom for future multi-change architecture migrations.
documents.agreement:
# Agreement — completeStateBackedGate

## Update history
- 2026-05-29: Validator surfaced archive-bundle materialization gap. User approved Path A (scope-expand). Added AC7 + clarified C1 scope.
- 2026-05-29: Mid-design, found `readAgreement` (contract.ts) is the lone disk-first artifact reader (vs Temporal-first `readArtifact`). User approved folding the fix in. Added AC8; C4 broadened.
- 2026-05-29: Related-scan ("anything else not using temporal store properly") found the ACTUAL root cause of the stale-contract symptom: `store-temporal/changes.ts updateArtifacts` does not invalidate `changeCache` after firing content signals, unlike every other mutation method. User approved folding in. Added AC9; C4 broadened.

## Objectives

1. Migrate the acceptance gate's non-recovery readiness check from disk inspection (`inspectArtifactActivity`) to state-backed evidence — completing the architectural transition started by `fixGateArtifactReadiness`.
2. Preserve the acceptance recovery path (poisoned-history) on `inspectArtifactActivity` per C12 of `removePositionalArtifactApi`.
3. Achieve parity across all four artifact-backed gates (proposal, discovery, design, acceptance) on the non-recovery path.
4. Preserve archive-bundle materialization of executive-summary so post-archive readers continue to find the artifact.
5. Make `readAgreement` (contract minting) Temporal-first for consistency with the canonical `readArtifact` reader.
6. Fix `updateArtifacts` cache invalidation so post-`adv_change_update` reads return fresh artifact content (root cause of stale-contract symptom).

## Acceptance Criteria

- **AC1 — Non-recovery acceptance state-backed.** When `state.documents.executiveSummary` is non-empty AND `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.{path,contentHash}` are set, `adv_gate_complete gateId: "acceptance"` advances regardless of pre-existing disk-file presence. Verified by integration test that creates a change, runs it through gates without prior disk-writes for executive-summary, asserts acceptance completes.

- **AC2 — Recovery path disk inspection preserved.** When acceptance gate completion uses `recoveryMode: "poisoned_history"`, `inspectArtifactActivity` continues to verify disk content against `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.contentHash`. Verified by replay test exercising the existing recovery flow.

- **AC3 — Replay safety.** A versioned patch marker is added around the workflow-side change. Old histories replay deterministically; new histories use the new path. Verified by deterministic-replay test.

- **AC4 — Legacy compatibility.** Existing changes that pre-date `removePositionalArtifactApi` and have on-disk acceptance artifacts continue to complete acceptance cleanly. Verified by a regression test fixture.

- **AC5 — Test coverage.** New regression test asserts no-disk acceptance completion through the full workflow signal path. Existing `fixGateArtifactReadiness` pattern tests extended to cover acceptance.

- **AC6 — Documentation parity.** The `fixGateArtifactReadiness` recovery note (acceptance carve-out) is updated to reflect the completed migration. Inline comments in `workflows.ts` explain state-backed vs recovery-path division.

- **AC7 — Archive-bundle executive-summary materialization.** At acceptance gate completion (inside the new patched branch), `state.documents.executiveSummary` is written to disk at `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.path` via `writeArtifactActivity`, mirroring the existing acceptance.md projection write at workflows.ts:803. Ensures `createArchive`/`createInRepoArchive` include executive-summary.md in the bundle. Verified by integration test through acceptance + archive asserting `_executiveSummary` resolves from bundle.

- **AC8 — readAgreement Temporal-first.** `readAgreement` in `tools/contract.ts` reads Temporal `state.documents.agreement` FIRST, falling back to disk then archive — matching `readArtifact` ordering. Ideally delegates to `readArtifact(store, change.id, "agreement")`. Verified by unit test: Temporal-fresh + disk-stale agreement → minted contract uses Temporal content.

- **AC9 — updateArtifacts cache invalidation.** `store-temporal/changes.ts updateArtifacts` calls `invalidateChange(changeId)` after the content-signal fan-out (before return), matching `save`/`close`/`refresh`/bulk-close. Ensures a `store.changes.get(changeId)` immediately following `adv_change_update` returns fresh `state.documents`/`state.artifacts` rather than stale cached content. Verified by a store-level test: update artifact content, then immediate `get`, assert returned `documents[kind]` equals the new content (RED today: returns stale cached value).

## Constraints

- **C1 — No production-write-flow disk dual-write.** MUST NOT restore disk writes for `agreement`/`design`/`executiveSummary`/`acceptance` in the artifact-update write path (`store-temporal/changes.ts` fireContentSignalsSequentially). `no-disk-writes-invariant.test.ts` continues to hold. AC7's archive-time materialization is a separate lifecycle (one-time at acceptance gate), identical in pattern to the existing acceptance.md write at workflows.ts:803.

- **C2 — `inspectArtifactActivity` interface unchanged.** Continues to exist and remains used by recovery path + archive bundle materialization. Only its USE SITE in the non-recovery acceptance gate changes.

- **C3 — Workflow replay determinism.** All workflow code changes gated behind a `patched` marker. The new `writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary)` (AC7) sits inside the new patched branch.

- **C4 — Bounded scope.** Scope limited to: (a) acceptance non-recovery readiness predicate, (b) archive-bundle composition (AC7), (c) `readAgreement` Temporal-first fix (AC8), (d) `updateArtifacts` cache-invalidation fix (AC9). Do NOT touch the recovery path. Do NOT touch `inspectArtifactActivity`'s implementation. Do NOT broaden AC8/AC9 into a sweeping reader/cache refactor — AC8 is `readAgreement` only; AC9 is the single missing `invalidateChange` call in `updateArtifacts`.

- **C5 — Worktree isolation (P32).** All edits via the change worktree; no trunk edits.

- **C6 — Test-driven.** RED evidence first; GREEN implementation; targeted + full test pass before review.

## Avoidances (Out of Scope)

- **A1 — Production-write-flow disk writes.** Not restoring.
- **A2 — `inspectArtifactActivity` internals.** Not refactoring.
- **A3 — Archive-bundle materialization architecture.** Not changing the `createArchive` readdir mechanism; we feed it the file.
- **A4 — Recovery path semantics.** Disk inspection there is intentional (C12).
- **A5 — Disk-write workaround tooling.** Not productizing.
- **A6 — Cross-project migrations.** Out of surface.
- **A7 — Sweeping reader refactor.** AC8 fixes `readAgreement` only; `change.ts` readers already Temporal-first; `roadmap.ts`/`project.ts`/`conformance.ts`/`worktree` read non-artifact config, out of scope.
- **A8 — Sweeping cache-discipline refactor.** AC9 fixes the single `updateArtifacts` invalidation gap. `create` does not need it (new change, no stale cache). Other mutation methods already self-invalidate.

## Validation Strategy

- RED first: (a) no-disk acceptance flow fails with `ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_MISSING`; (b) AC8 Temporal-fresh+disk-stale mints from disk (wrong); (c) AC9 update-then-get returns stale documents.
- GREEN: predicate swap + archive materialization + readAgreement inversion + updateArtifacts invalidation; all tests pass.
- AC7 verification: integration test through acceptance + archive.
- Replay determinism: existing histories complete unchanged (patch marker).
- Full `pnpm test`, `pnpm run check`, `pnpm run build` pass.
- Independent acceptance reviewer pass.

## Research Provenance

- `fixGateArtifactReadiness` (advance, archived 2026-05-28): partial state-backed migration; acceptance carve-out
- `removePositionalArtifactApi` (advance, archived 2026-05-28): removed disk writes; AC11 no-disk-write invariant
- `addPerProjectOcWrapper` (toolbox, 2026-05-28) D12: `inspectArtifactActivity` XDG-aware
- Reproducibility 2026-05-28→29: `fixOcE2eTestBugs`, `fixLauncherPostMigration`, pokeedge `fixSubDollarLabels`
- Design validation 2026-05-29: `adv-researcher` surfaced archive-bundle gap → Path A (AC7)
- readAgreement finding 2026-05-29: disk-first outlier → AC8
- updateArtifacts cache finding 2026-05-29: root cause of stale-contract symptom (cache not invalidated after content signals) → AC9

Snapshot date: **2026-05-29**.
documents.design:
# Design — completeStateBackedGate (revised 2026-05-29, 3 folded-in findings)

## Goal

Complete the state-backed-canonical transition for the acceptance gate, plus two related Temporal-store correctness fixes surfaced during design/related-scan:
1. Acceptance gate non-recovery readiness → state-backed (AC1-6)
2. Archive-bundle executive-summary materialization (AC7)
3. `readAgreement` Temporal-first (AC8)
4. `updateArtifacts` cache invalidation — root cause of stale-contract symptom (AC9)

## Architecture — acceptance gate (AC1-7)

### Current (broken)

```
gateCompleted(acceptance) → evaluateGateReadiness
  L1 acceptanceContractBlockers: contract + reviewMatrix + state.artifacts.executiveSummary.{path,contentHash}  ✓ (metadata signal populates)
  ↓ ready, evidence=undefined
artifactKind==="acceptance" && !evidence → projectionChangesDir branch
  ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH on:
    writeArtifactActivity(acceptance) → disk           [KEEP - archive bundle]
    state.documents.acceptance = content               [KEEP]
    inspectArtifactActivity(executiveSummary)  ← BREAKS: disk file never written
      → ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_MISSING
```

### Target (state-backed + AC7 materialization)

```
gateCompleted(acceptance) → evaluateGateReadiness  [L1 unchanged]
  ↓ ready, evidence=undefined
ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH on:
  writeArtifactActivity(acceptance) → disk            [KEEP, outside new patch]
  state.documents.acceptance = content                [KEEP, outside new patch]
  wf.patched(STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH):
    TRUE (new):
      1. verify state.documents.executiveSummary present + size ≥ MIN
      2. writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary, state.documents.executiveSummary)  [AC7 - archive bundle]
         → block ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_WRITE_FAILED on !ok
      3. stateBackedArtifactEvidence(state, "acceptance", "acceptance", now)  → artifactEvidence
      4. skip trailing inspectArtifactActivity(artifactKind)
    FALSE (legacy): existing inspectArtifactActivity(executiveSummary) + hash-match block (lines 833-883)
                    + trailing inspectArtifactActivity(acceptance)
```

### Recovery path (gate.ts:343-414) — UNCHANGED (C2/C4)

## Key Decisions

### KD-1: `STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH = "state-backed-acceptance-proof-v1"`
New versioned marker (4th in the file). Temporal best practice: each substantive command-sequence change = own logical patch (https://docs.temporal.io/develop/typescript/versioning#patching). Not expanding `STATE_BACKED_GATE_ARTIFACT_PROOF_PATCH` because histories overlap differently. Validator-confirmed.

### KD-2: Reuse `stateBackedArtifactEvidence` (gate-readiness.ts:97-146)
Acceptance shares the same content+metadata shape as proposal/discovery/design. Helper drops in unmodified. For acceptance evidence, call with `("acceptance","acceptance")` reading the just-populated `state.documents.acceptance`; `state.artifacts.acceptance` is absent (acceptance is a derived projection, not metadata-tracked) — helper handles absent path/contentHash gracefully (mirrors design test case gate-readiness.test.ts:116-141).

### KD-3: AC7 `writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary)` inside the new patch
Materializes `state.documents.executiveSummary` → disk so `createArchive`/`createInRepoArchive` (readdir copy) include it. C1-compliant: production artifact-update flow stays disk-free (`no-disk-writes-invariant.test.ts` holds); this is one-time archive-lifecycle materialization, mirroring the existing acceptance.md write at workflows.ts:803.

### KD-4: Block ordering
Lines 803-832 (acceptance projection write + `state.documents.acceptance`) stay OUTSIDE the new patch. Lines 833-883 (executiveSummary inspection) wrapped by patched/legacy branches. Trailing `inspectArtifactActivity(artifactKind)` block (885-925) gated on `!artifactEvidence` → runs legacy, skipped state-backed.

### KD-5: AC8 — readAgreement Temporal-first (tools/contract.ts:91-106)
Replace the disk-first body with delegation to the canonical reader:
```ts
async function readAgreement(store: Store, change: Change): Promise<string> {
  assertSafeChangeId(change.id);
  const content = await readArtifact(store, change.id, "agreement"); // Temporal→disk→bundle
  if (content?.trim()) return content;
  throw new Error(`Agreement artifact is empty: ${change.id}`);
}
```
`readArtifact` is exported from `tools/change.ts`. Import it. Removes the duplicate disk-first reader. RED test: Temporal state has fresh agreement, disk has different stale content → assert mint uses Temporal content. (Note: requires `readArtifact` import into contract.ts — check for circular import; if change.ts→contract.ts cycle exists, extract readArtifact to a shared module or inline the Temporal-first ordering directly in readAgreement.)

### KD-6: AC9 — updateArtifacts cache invalidation (store-temporal/changes.ts:610-696)
Single missing call. After the `runTemporal(... fireContentSignalsSequentially ...)` block (line 675) and before composing/returning `result`, add:
```ts
invalidateChange(changeId);
```
`invalidateChange` is already imported (line 116) and used by save/close/refresh/bulkClose. This drops the stale `changeCache` entry so the next `store.changes.get(changeId)` re-queries Temporal for fresh `documents`/`artifacts`. RED test (store-level): call `updateArtifacts(changeId, { agreement: NEW })`, then `store.changes.get(changeId)`, assert `documents.agreement === NEW` (today returns stale cached value).

**Why AC9 is the load-bearing fix:** the observed stale-contract symptom (mint persisted 12-item contract from old agreement while state had 13-item new) was caused by this missing invalidation, NOT by readAgreement's disk-first ordering (disk file was absent so the fallback fired correctly — it read `change.documents.agreement`, which was the STALE CACHED value). AC8 is defense-in-depth and architectural consistency; AC9 fixes the actual data-staleness.

## Files Changed

| File | Change | AC |
|---|---|---|
| `plugin/src/temporal/workflows.ts` | `STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH` const + rationale comment; wrap 833-883 in patched/legacy; patched branch does writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary) + stateBackedArtifactEvidence; skip trailing inspect when patched | AC1,3,6,7 |
| `plugin/src/tools/contract.ts` | `readAgreement` → Temporal-first (delegate to `readArtifact` or inline Temporal-first ordering) | AC8 |
| `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.ts` | `updateArtifacts`: add `invalidateChange(changeId)` after signal fan-out | AC9 |
| `plugin/src/temporal/workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts` | no-disk acceptance completion; writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary) fires; write-failure blocker | AC1,5,7 |
| `plugin/src/tools/contract.test.ts` | Temporal-fresh + disk-stale agreement → mint uses Temporal | AC8 |
| `plugin/src/storage/store-temporal/changes.test.ts` | update-then-get returns fresh documents | AC9 |
| `plugin/src/temporal/__tests__/replay/` | verify existing fixtures pass (legacy branch); add fixture if acceptance legacy path uncovered | AC4 |
| `plugin/src/archive/archive.test.ts` | through acceptance + archive, bundle contains executive-summary.md | AC7 |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | new patch marker + completed transition + AC7/AC8/AC9 | AC6 |
| `docs/temporal-recovery.md` | recovery-path note update if needed | AC6 |

## Test Strategy

RED → GREEN per AC. Targeted:
```bash
bin/oc-test targeted -- src/temporal/workflows.signal-handlers.test.ts src/temporal/gate-readiness.test.ts src/tools/contract.test.ts src/storage/store-temporal/changes.test.ts
bin/oc-test full   # or pnpm test --maxWorkers=4 if bin/oc-test absent
pnpm run check && pnpm run build
```

## Replay Determinism Statement

- `wf.patched("state-backed-acceptance-proof-v1")` — new histories record marker; old don't.
- Legacy histories → `else` branch (inspectArtifactActivity sequence) → identical replay.
- New histories → patched branch: command sequence `writeArtifactActivity(acceptance)` → `writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary)`; `stateBackedArtifactEvidence` is a pure state read (no commands).
- `writeArtifactActivity(acceptance)` sits outside the new patch → schedule unchanged across branches.
- AC8/AC9 are tool/store-layer (non-workflow) — no replay impact.

## Risks

| Risk | Mitigation |
|---|---|
| readArtifact import into contract.ts creates circular dep | If cycle, inline Temporal-first ordering in readAgreement instead of importing (KD-5 fallback). Verify with typecheck. |
| AC9 invalidation masks a deeper cache bug | Store-level RED test pins the exact behavior; full suite catches regressions. |
| writeArtifactActivity(executiveSummary) failure blocks acceptance | New blocker ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_WRITE_FAILED; same failure mode as existing acceptance.md write. |
| New patch marker confuses future migrations | Documented rationale block; chronological sequencing. |
| Legacy replay regression | wf.patched false for unrecorded markers; existing fixtures cover. |

## Out of Scope
Per C4 + A1-A8: no production disk-write restoration; no inspectArtifactActivity refactor; no archive-mechanism change; no recovery-path change; no proposal/discovery/design predicate change; no sweeping reader or cache-discipline refactor.
documents.executiveSummary:
# Executive Summary — completeStateBackedGate

## Outcome
The ADV acceptance gate's non-recovery completion path is now fully state-backed, completing the Temporal-canonical migration begun by `removePositionalArtifactApi` and `fixGateArtifactReadiness`. Acceptance now derives its executive-summary proof from workflow state (`state.documents.executiveSummary` + `state.artifacts.executiveSummary.{path,contentHash}`) instead of inspecting a disk file that the Temporal-only store no longer writes. This unblocks acceptance for every change created after the disk-write removal.

## What was built
- **State-backed acceptance branch** (`workflows.ts`): new `STATE_BACKED_ACCEPTANCE_PROOF_PATCH` marker wraps a branch that reads proof from state via the new deterministic `stateBackedAcceptanceProof()` helper (`gate-readiness.ts`) — no `inspectArtifactActivity` disk read. The legacy `ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH` disk-inspect branch is retained solely for replay of pre-migration histories, evaluated after the new marker so old histories fall through deterministically.
- **Archive-bundle materialization (AC7)**: at acceptance the workflow writes `executive-summary.md` and `acceptance.md` to the active change dir via `writeArtifactActivity` (proxy kind union extended with `executiveSummary`), so the readdir-based `createInRepoArchive` includes them in the bundle.
- **readAgreement Temporal-first (AC8)**: `contract.ts` `readAgreement` now delegates to `readArtifact(store, id, "agreement")` (Temporal → disk → archive), eliminating the lone disk-first reader outlier. No import cycle.
- **updateArtifacts cache invalidation (AC9)**: `store-temporal/changes.ts` `updateArtifacts` now calls `invalidateChange(changeId)` after the content-signal fan-out — the confirmed root cause of the stale-contract read-after-write symptom (re-mint required after `adv_change_update`).

## What was verified
- **Tests**: TDD red→green across all 3 fix surfaces (10 tasks). New no-disk acceptance integration test, AC8 Temporal-fresh-over-stale-disk test, AC9 cache-invalidation test, AC3 patch-ordering guard, AC4/AC7 archive-bundle tests. Existing acceptance pattern tests migrated to the state-backed model. Full suite `pnpm test --maxWorkers=4` exit 0; `pnpm run check` exit 0; `pnpm run build` exit 0 (worker bundle compiled clean).
- **Scope (C4)**: `git diff` confirms recovery path (`gate.ts`, `_recovery-writers.ts`) and `inspectArtifactActivity` (`activities.ts`) have zero diff. 9 files touched total.
- **Replay safety (AC3/C3)**: patch-marker ordering invariant guarded by test; workflow-bundle-boundary + determinism guards pass.

## Remaining concerns
None blocking. The legacy `ACCEPTANCE_EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY_PROOF_PATCH` branch can be removed via `wf.deprecatePatch` once pre-migration acceptance histories are archived/closed and replay fixtures no longer cover the disk-inspect path (documented deprecation plan in workflows.ts).
